encryption_algorithm: AES-256-CTR (key wrapped with RSA-4096 public key — decryption requires attacker private key)
anomaly_signals: mass_rename_rate: 312 file renames/second — no legitimate process performs bulk renames at this rate
ransomware_extension: .AIIMS_LOCKED extension — targeted ransomware, customized to victim organization name
3am_deployment: 3:00 AM IST — maximum impact, minimum immediate response staff
spawned_from_persistence: svhost32.exe (note: fake svchost spelling) launched from Day 3 persistence mechanism
all_shares_targeted: 6 different network paths — attacker mapped all shares over 22 days before deploying
hospital_critical_impact: patient records, HIS database encrypted — clinical operations immediately halted
ground_truth_note: MALICIOUS: Ransomware deployment. The customized '.AIIMS_LOCKED' extension confirms this is a targeted attack — the ransomware binary was compiled specifically for AIIMS Delhi, not a commodity ransomware campaign. The 312 files/second rename rate means 284,712 patient records were encrypted in ~15 minutes. The process name 'svhost32.exe' is a deliberate typo of the legitimate 'svchost.exe' — a last-minute masquerade. It's spawned by the same persistence task established 19 days ago on Day 3. This is the culmination of a 22-day operation.

file_content_excerpt:
YOUR FILES HAVE BEEN ENCRYPTED

All patient records, administrative documents, and research data at AIIMS Delhi have been encrypted with military-grade encryption.

To restore your systems:
1. Do NOT attempt to decrypt files yourself — this will cause permanent data loss
2. Do NOT contact law enforcement — this will result in publication of patient data
3. Contact us within 72 hours: aiims_recovery@protonmail.com

Proof of decryption available for 5 sample files.
Ransom amount: USD 6,500,000 in Bitcoin
Bitcoin address: bc1qxy2kgdygjrsqtzq2n0yrf4485p3tqmqkqdaqy

DEADLINE: 72 HOURS

Data leaked if payment not received:
- 284,712 patient records including HIV/AIDS, psychiatric, oncology
- 15 years of AIIMS research data
- Staff salary and HR records
- Administrative credentials and network maps

We have already exfiltrated all data. Payment prevents publication.
anomaly_signals: ransom_note_mass_write: 8441 instances of READ_THIS_AIIMS.txt written — one in every directory
protonmail_contact: encrypted email service for anonymized attacker communication
bitcoin_demand: $6.5M ransom — consistent with nation-state-linked groups' healthcare targeting demands
data_leak_threat: confirms exfiltration already occurred (consistent with Day 12 data exfiltration we detected)
aiims_specific: note written specifically for AIIMS — targeted attack confirmation
ground_truth_note: MALICIOUS: The ransom note confirms the double-extortion model — data was already exfiltrated (consistent with the 2.3GB exfiltration detected on Day 12) AND systems are now encrypted. The $6.5M demand and the threat to publish HIV/AIDS, psychiatric, and oncology patient records (maximum sensitivity data) confirm this is a sophisticated, targeted attack with pre-planned leverage. The Bitcoin address and Protonmail contact method are consistent with APT41-affiliated financial extortion operations.

command_used: net stop "Block Level Backup Engine Service" /y && sc config wbengine start= disabled
anomaly_signals: backup_service_killed: Windows Backup Engine stopped — eliminates automated backup recovery
service_disabled: sc config start=disabled prevents service restart — persistent disablement
simultaneous_with_encryption: executed in parallel with file encryption at 3:02 AM
backup_server_targeted: attacker targeting the backup server specifically — shows awareness of recovery paths
ground_truth_note: MALICIOUS: The attacker stops and disables the Windows Block Level Backup Engine — the primary backup mechanism for the backup server. Combined with the Day 7 VSS deletion, this completes a comprehensive destruction of all recovery paths: (1) Volume Shadow Copies deleted Day 7, (2) Backup service disabled Day 22, (3) Files encrypted Day 22. Any attempt to recover via Windows backup mechanisms will fail. This pre-planned, multi-stage destruction of recovery capabilities is a hallmark of APT41's 'maximum impact' ransomware playbook.

command_line: bcdedit /set {default} recoveryenabled No && bcdedit /set {default} bootstatuspolicy ignoreallfailures
anomaly_signals: recovery_disabled: Windows Recovery Environment disabled — prevents boot-time file recovery
bootpolicy_override: ignoreallfailures hides boot errors — system appears to boot but OS is broken
dc_targeted: executed on domain controller — domain infrastructure recovery disabled
triple_recovery_elimination: 3rd recovery mechanism destroyed (VSS Day7 + WBEngine Day22 + WRE Day22)
ground_truth_note: MALICIOUS: bcdedit disables Windows Recovery Environment on the Domain Controller. Combined with VSS deletion (Day 7) and backup service stop, this is the third pillar of a comprehensive recovery prevention strategy. The 'bootstatuspolicy ignoreallfailures' setting means even if the OS fails to boot properly, it won't enter recovery mode — it will just silently fail. AIIMS IT staff would need bare-metal restore from external backup to recover the domain controller. With the DCs encrypted, all domain authentication fails, bringing all domain-joined systems offline.

lockout_mechanism: Automated bad-password spray against all admin accounts — deliberate lockout to prevent emergency response
anomaly_signals: simultaneous_lockout: all 5 domain admin accounts locked out at same second — impossible organically
response_disruption: locking admin accounts at 3 AM prevents IT staff from logging in to respond
deliberate_spray: 5 bad passwords × 5 accounts in <1 second — automated account lockout weapon
dc_as_source: lockout originated from DC itself — attacker has DC SYSTEM access, operating locally
discovery_context: AIIMS system administrators discovered encryption at approximately 07:00 IST when morning shift staff found systems unresponsive. Domain admin accounts were locked, requiring physical data center access and AD recovery procedures. The 4-hour gap between encryption (03:00) and discovery (07:00) eliminated any chance of hot-containment.
ground_truth_note: MALICIOUS: Final act — the attacker simultaneously locks all domain admin accounts by triggering account lockout policy through rapid bad-password attempts. This is the 'scorched earth' final move: even if an IT admin was watching at 3 AM, they cannot log in to the domain to stop the ransomware. Simultaneous lockout of all admin accounts in the same second is impossible via organic user behavior — this is automated. AIIMS IT staff discovered the attack at 07:00 IST, 4 hours after deployment, when morning shift staff found systems encrypted and accounts locked.
